prompt: Where does the claim that we make about 35,000 decisions a day come from?

Where does the rule that it takes 12 positive comments to outweigh a single criticism come from?
Where does the figure that money stops buying happiness past about $75,000 a year come from?
What is the origin of the claim that the human body contains enough iron to make a three-inch nail?
Where does the claim that the DNA in one human body could stretch to the sun and back hundreds of times come from?
Where does the claim that it takes about 200 muscles to take a single step come from?
Where does the statistic that half of all marriages end in divorce come from?
What is the origin of the Mozart-effect claim that listening to Mozart raises your IQ by several points?
Where does the claim that multitasking can cut your productivity by about 40% come from?
What is the source of the claim that office workers are only truly productive for about three hours a day?
Where does the figure that it costs around $233,000 to raise a child to age eighteen come from?
Where does the claim that each cigarette takes about 11 minutes off your life come from?
What is the origin of the statistic that the average office worker sends and receives around 121 emails a day?
Where does the saying that you are seven times more likely to die driving to buy a lottery ticket than to win it come from?
Where does the claim that a person spends roughly 90,000 hours at work over a lifetime come from?
What is the origin of the story that spinach was thought to be rich in iron because of a misplaced decimal point?
Where does the claim that Napoleon was unusually short at about five feet two come from?

What is the origin of the advice to replace running shoes every 300 to 500 miles?
Where does the belief that you must wait 24 hours before reporting someone missing come from?
Where does the recommendation that adults need exactly eight hours of sleep a night come from?
What is the origin of the 2,000-calories-a-day figure printed on nutrition labels?
Where does the claim that reading to a child 20 minutes a day exposes them to nearly two million words a year come from?
Where does the 80/20 rule that 80% of results come from 20% of the effort come from?

Where does the figure that we share about 98% of our DNA with chimpanzees come from?
Where does the claim that children laugh hundreds of times a day while adults laugh only about fifteen come from?
